files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/app/api/routes/health.py b/app/api/routes/health.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/health.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/health.py
@@ -7,12 +7,16 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import httpx
+import time
+from datetime import datetime
 
 from fastapi import APIRouter
 
 from app.core.config import get_settings
 from app.core.logging import get_logger
 from app.rag.vector_store import ChromaStore
+
+START_TIME = time.time()
 
 logger = get_logger(__name__)
 router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/health", tags=["health"])
@@ -47,11 +51,14 @@
         logger.warning("ChromaDB health check failed: %s", exc)
 
     status = "ok" if (ollama_ok and chroma_ok) else "degraded"
+    uptime_seconds = int(time.time() - START_TIME)
+    
     return {
         "status": status,
         "ollama": ollama_ok,
         "chromadb": chroma_ok,
         "llm_model": settings.OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL,
         "embed_model": settings.OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL,
+        "uptime": f"{uptime_seconds}s",
+        "server_time": datetime.now().isoformat(),
     }

scenarios.title: Health check returns OK when all dependencies are healthy
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' with all dependency flags true when both Ollama and ChromaDB are reachable and functioning correctly.
preconditions: Ollama service is running and accessible at configured OLLAMA_BASE_URL
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200 with JSON body containing status 'ok', ollama: true, chromadb: true, and populated llm_model field.

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when Ollama is unreachable
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with ollama: false when the Ollama service is not reachable.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or OLLAMA_BASE_URL is misconfigured
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200 with status 'degraded', ollama: false, chromadb: true. Warning logged for Ollama health check failure.

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when ChromaDB fails to initialize
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with chromadb: false when ChromaDB collection initialization fails.
preconditions: ChromaDB is misconfigured or its storage is corrupted/inaccessible
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200 with status 'degraded', ollama: true, chromadb: false. Warning logged for ChromaDB health check failure.

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded when both dependencies fail
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with all dependency flags false when both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or unreachable
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200 with status 'degraded', ollama: false, chromadb: false. Both failure warnings logged.

scenarios.title: Health check handles Ollama timeout gracefully
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check endpoint handles Ollama connection timeout without blocking indefinitely, respecting the 3-second timeout configured in httpx.AsyncClient.
preconditions: Ollama service is slow to respond (more than 3 seconds) or network introduces delay
scenarios.expected_result: Health check completes within reasonable time (under 5s), returns HTTP 200 with ollama: false. Timeout exception is caught and logged as warning.

scenarios.title: Health check response includes correct LLM model from settings
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check response accurately reflects the configured OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value from application settings.
preconditions: Application is configured with a specific OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value
scenarios.expected_result: Response llm_model field contains the exact value configured in OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL setting.

scenarios.title: Health check handles concurrent requests without race conditions
scenarios.description: Verify that multiple simultaneous health check requests are handled correctly without causing race conditions in ChromaStore initialization or httpx client creation.
scenarios.expected_result: All 10 concurrent requests return HTTP 200 with identical, consistent response bodies. No exceptions or race conditions occur.

scenarios.title: Health check endpoint accessible without authentication
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check endpoint is intentionally public and accessible without authentication headers, as it is typically used by infrastructure for readiness probes.
preconditions: Application may have authentication enabled for other endpoints
scenarios.expected_result: Health check endpoint returns HTTP 200 without requiring authentication, confirming it is intentionally public for infrastructure monitoring.

scenarios.title: Health check validates response schema compliance
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check response adheres to the expected JSON schema with all required fields present and correctly typed.
scenarios.expected_result: Response has correct Content-Type header and JSON body with all fields having correct types (strings for status/llm_model, booleans for ollama/chromadb).

scenarios.title: Health check handles ChromaDB count operation failure
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check gracefully handles ChromaDB client initialization succeeding but count() method failing due to collection issues.
preconditions: ChromaDB client initializes but collection operations fail (e.g., corrupted collection, permissions issue)
scenarios.expected_result: Health check returns HTTP 200 with chromadb: false. Exception from store.count() is caught and logged as warning.

generated_specs.content:
import os
import pytest
import json
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect, sync_playwright

BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")
HEALTH_ENDPOINT = "/api/health"


def _get_health_url():
    return f"{BASE_URL}{HEALTH_ENDPOINT}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 741e718d-6524-454e-8ce6-91670cdd5578
def test_health_check_returns_ok_when_all_dependencies_healthy(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' with all dependency flags true."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    
    expected_status = "ok"
    assert data["status"] == expected_status, f"Expected status '{expected_status}', got '{data.get('status')}'"
    assert data["ollama"] is True, "Expected ollama to be True"
    assert data["chromadb"] is True, "Expected chromadb to be True"
    assert "llm_model" in data, "Expected llm_model field to be present"
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str), "Expected llm_model to be a string"
    assert len(data["llm_model"]) > 0, "Expected llm_model to be non-empty"


# SCENARIO_ID: dabc2ae8-31c4-455e-a65d-6aa0ff077694
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_ollama_unreachable(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with ollama: false when Ollama is unreachable."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    
    assert data["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got '{data.get('status')}'"
    assert data["ollama"] is False, "Expected ollama to be False when Ollama is unreachable"
    assert data["chromadb"] is True, "Expected chromadb to be True when ChromaDB is functioning"


# SCENARIO_ID: 0a5aec7d-fa08-4853-b8a6-072e3a95bc53
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_chromadb_fails(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with chromadb: false when ChromaDB fails."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    
    assert data["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got '{data.get('status')}'"
    assert data["ollama"] is True, "Expected ollama to be True when Ollama is running"
    assert data["chromadb"] is False, "Expected chromadb to be False when ChromaDB initialization fails"


# SCENARIO_ID: 985041a1-95ec-4f47-8a58-6758456b83fe
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_both_dependencies_fail(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with all dependency flags false."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    
    assert data["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got '{data.get('status')}'"
    assert data["ollama"] is False, "Expected ollama to be False"
    assert data["chromadb"] is False, "Expected chromadb to be False"


# SCENARIO_ID: ba61d3cf-9f8a-41a0-88e5-7667aae03d7f
def test_health_check_handles_ollama_timeout_gracefully(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health check endpoint handles Ollama connection timeout without blocking indefinitely."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    max_response_time_seconds = 5
    
    start_time = time.time()
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    assert elapsed_time < max_response_time_seconds, (
        f"Response took {elapsed_time:.2f}s, expected less than {max_response_time_seconds}s"
    )
    
    data = response.json()
    assert data["ollama"] is False, "Expected ollama to be False on timeout"


# SCENARIO_ID: 36df6c9d-d936-467c-8863-2e628743d8ef
def test_health_check_includes_correct_llm_model_from_settings(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health check response accurately reflects the configured OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    expected_model_env = os.environ.get("OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL", "")
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    assert "llm_model" in data, "Expected llm_model field in response"
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str), "Expected llm_model to be a string"
    
    if expected_model_env:
        assert data["llm_model"] == expected_model_env, (
            f"Expected llm_model '{expected_model_env}', got '{data['llm_model']}'"
        )


# SCENARIO_ID: 5353df04-1b98-463d-97ab-c384d6f73ba2
def test_health_check_handles_concurrent_requests_without_race_conditions(page: Page):
    """Verify that multiple simultaneous health check requests are handled correctly without race conditions."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    concurrent_request_count = 10
    responses = []
    errors = []
    
    def make_health_request():
        with sync_playwright() as p:
            browser = p.chromium.launch()
            context = browser.new_context()
            page = context.new_page()
            try:
                response = page.request.get(health_url)
                data = response.json()
                browser.close()
                return {"status": response.status, "body": data}
            except Exception as e:
                browser.close()
                return {"error": str(e)}
    
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=concurrent_request_count) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(make_health_request) for _ in range(concurrent_request_count)]
        for future in as_completed(futures):
            result = future.result()
            if "error" in result:
                errors.append(result["error"])
            else:
                responses.append(result)
    
    assert len(errors) == 0, f"Encountered errors during concurrent requests: {errors}"
    assert len(responses) == concurrent_request_count, (
        f"Expected {concurrent_request_count} responses, got {len(responses)}"
    )
    
    first_status = responses[0]["body"]["status"]
    first_llm_model = responses[0]["body"]["llm_model"]
    
    for i, resp in enumerate(responses):
        assert resp["status"] == 200, f"Request {i}: Expected status 200, got {resp['status']}"
        assert resp["body"]["status"] == first_status, (
            f"Request {i}: Inconsistent status '{resp['body']['status']}' vs expected '{first_status}'"
        )
        assert resp["body"]["llm_model"] == first_llm_model, (
            f"Request {i}: Inconsistent llm_model '{resp['body']['llm_model']}' vs expected '{first_llm_model}'"
        )


# SCENARIO_ID: 546b30b0-4846-4657-aa72-b96cda51950b
def test_health_check_accessible_without_authentication(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health check endpoint is intentionally public and accessible without authentication."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200 without authentication, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    required_fields = ["status", "ollama", "chromadb", "llm_model"]
    for field in required_fields:
        assert field in data, f"Expected '{field}' field in response body"


# SCENARIO_ID: 450593ea-63c0-4202-8a82-742eeb62147e
def test_health_check_validates_response_schema_compliance(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health check response adheres to the expected JSON schema."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
    assert "application/json" in content_type, (
        f"Expected Content-Type to contain 'application/json', got '{content_type}'"
    )
    
    data = response.json()
    
    assert "status" in data, "Expected 'status' field in response"
    assert isinstance(data["status"], str), "Expected 'status' to be a string"
    assert data["status"] in ("ok", "degraded"), (
        f"Expected status to be 'ok' or 'degraded', got '{data['status']}'"
    )
    
    assert "ollama" in data, "Expected 'ollama' field in response"
    assert isinstance(data["ollama"], bool), "Expected 'ollama' to be a boolean"
    
    assert "chromadb" in data, "Expected 'chromadb' field in response"
    assert isinstance(data["chromadb"], bool), "Expected 'chromadb' to be a boolean"
    
    assert "llm_model" in data, "Expected 'llm_model' field in response"
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str), "Expected 'llm_model' to be a string"


# SCENARIO_ID: be4357e0-abfa-4f39-a91a-8eb03b8845cb
def test_health_check_handles_chromadb_count_operation_failure(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health check gracefully handles ChromaDB count operation failure."""
    health_url = _get_health_url()
    
    response = page.request.get(health_url)
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    data = response.json()
    assert data["chromadb"] is False, "Expected chromadb to be False when count operation fails"
